calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-02-2026 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-10-2025 Continued To Apr-29-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Litigation Pending Appeal Of Denial Of Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum; Declaration Of Jonathan D. Gottlieb
Off Calendar Oct-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RAISER, LLC, RAISER-CA, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Litigation Pending Appeal Of Denial Of Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum; Declaration Of Jonathan D. Gottlieb

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Raiser, LLC and Raiser-CA, LLC's unopposed Motion To Stay Litigation Pending Appeal Of Denial Of Motion To Compel Arbitration is GRANTED.

The court rejects Moving Defendants' argument that federal procedural rules govern. State procedural rules generally govern state court proceedings. On this motion, Moving Defendants have not shown that they and Plaintiff Raquel Babuglia are bound by an agreement to arbitrate, let alone an agreement in which the parties have "expressly designate[d] that any arbitration proceeding should move forward under the FAA's procedural provisions rather than under state procedural law." (Los Angeles Unified Sch. Dist. v Safety Nat'l Cas. Corp. (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 471, 475-476, 482, quoting Cronus Investments, Inc. v. Concierge Services (2005) 35 Cal.4th 376, 394.) State procedural law authorizes an appeal from an order denying a motion to compel arbitration but also provides "the perfecting of such an appeal shall not automatically stay any proceedings in the trial court during the pendency of the appeal." (Code of Civil Procedure section 1294(a).) Thus, here, there is no automatic stay by virtue of Moving Defendants' appeal from the order denying their motion to compel Plaintiff Babuglia to arbitrate her claims against them.

The court, however, exercises its discretion in favor of a stay. The court need not address Moving Defendants' contentions regarding prejudice and the balance of hardships because Plaintiff has expressly endorsed Moving Defendants' stay request. Nor by making this order is the court expressing any views regarding the merit of Moving Defendants' appeal.

Good cause appearing, this matter is stayed until the conclusion of Moving Defendants' appeal from the order denying their motion to compel Plaintiff Babuglia to arbitrate her claims against them or until further court order, whichever occurs first.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-14-2025 Continued To Oct-29-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Jul-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
June Bashant, Esq., of ROUDA, FEDER, TIETJEN & McGUINN , Tel:415-398-5398 for plaintiff RAQUEL BABUGLIA AND RENATO FRATTA.( in person appearance)
Sarah Pama, Esq., of MCNAMARA, AMBACHER,WHEELER,HIRSIG &GRAY LLP., Tel: (925) 939-5330 for Defendant AMIRHOSSEIN GHORABIAN.
Cristina Ciminelli, Esq., of Yukevich Cavanaugh., Tel: 2133627777 for
defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.
Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR# 13859, Tel: 714-471-2597 email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com

Hearing is held. The Court received a timely notice of contest via email; however, the notice did not specify the issues being contested. Counsel Cristina Ciminelli contests the tentative ruling concerning the third-party beneficiary in relation to the arbitration. The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RAISER, LLC, RAISER-CA, LLC) motion to compel arbitration is granted as to plaintiff Renato Fratta and denied as to plaintiff Raquel Babuglia. The court stays prosecution of Fratta's claims in this court pending arbitration. Defendants' motion to compel arbitration and stay of Babuglia's claims is denied. The parties are free to stipulate to stay the Fratta arbitration or seek that relief from the arbitrator while Babuglia litigates her claims.

See word minutes for complete ruling.

Order is signed.

Judge: Garrett L. Wong; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Court Reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR# 13859, Tel: 714-471-2597 email: justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com; Reported. (302/GLW)
